title: Carter County Superintendent of Schools (oversees Alzada)
notes: Tiny one-room Class C district (~8 students PK-8) in Alzada, MT. No on-site dedicated administrator (0.0 district admins per NCES). District is overseen by Carter County Superintendent of Schools Tracey Walker. School phone: (406) 828-4445. Address: 236 Spring Street, Alzada, MT 59311. County-supt is the only public-facing decision-maker; flag as low-priority for ECE outreach.

title: Lewis & Clark County Superintendent of Schools (oversaw Auchard Creek)
notes: Auchard Creek School (Wolf Creek, MT, ~16 students PK-8) CLOSED in 2024 per Public School Review. Was overseen by Lewis & Clark County Superintendent Katrina Chaney; the rural Class III districts (Auchard Creek, Wolf Creek, Canyon Creek) have no on-site administrators. Likely de-prioritize for ECE outreach since the school is closed.

title: Superintendent
notes: Class C K-12 district, Bainville, MT 59212. Superintendent confirmed via 'Meet the Superintendent' page on district site and ZoomInfo. Address: 409 Tubman St (PO Box 177).

title: County Superintendent (oversees Benton Lake)
notes: Tiny Class III rural district in Floweree/Cascade County (1 FTE teacher, 0 district admins per NCES). Has no dedicated district superintendent; falls under Cascade County Superintendent of Schools (Cascade County, MT). No named contact surfaced in public sources. Recommend Apollo enrichment or direct call to Cascade County Supt office.

title: Superintendent
notes: Bonner School District #14 (~338 students PK-8), Missoula County. Superintendent confirmed via ZoomInfo and LinkedIn. Email format inferred from domain (first-initial+lastname@bonner.k12.mt.us); confidence Medium pending verification. Address: 9045 Hwy 200, Bonner, MT 59823.

title: Richland County Superintendent of Schools (oversees Brorson)
notes: Small Class III K-6 country school outside Sidney, MT, in Richland County (1 FTE teacher). No dedicated district superintendent; supervised by Richland County Superintendent of Schools Dr. Timothy W. Tharp at 201 W. Main, Sidney, MT 59270. School direct phone: (406) 798-3361. Recommend Apollo enrichment for staff names.

title: Superintendent/Principal, Ekalaka Public Schools
notes: Carter County HS (Ekalaka) is the HS half of Ekalaka Public Schools (paired Elem+HS sharing Supt). James Kapptie signed contract March 2024 as combined Supt/Principal. Email inferred from district domain ekalaka.net; on-site email is obscured by spam-blocker but format jkapptie@ekalaka.net is the standard pattern. PO Box 458, Ekalaka, MT 59324.

title: Superintendent
notes: Centerville Public Schools (paired Elem+HS K-12 ~120 students), Sand Coulee, MT. Superintendent confirmed via district staff page; email format verified.

title: Superintendent
notes: Clancy Elementary School District (Jefferson County, MT). Daryl Mikesell started as Supt July 1, 2024 (previously teacher at Clancy, principal at Jefferson HS and Montana City School). Email format inferred from clancyschool.org domain pattern. Note: separate from Montana City School (Tony Kloker) which also serves Clancy area.

title: Head Teacher (district has no Supt; under Park County Supt Lisa Rosberg)
notes: Tiny one-room schoolhouse in Cooke City, Park County (~5 students PK-8). No district superintendent (0.50 district admins per NCES) - oversight from Park County Supt Lisa Rosberg (406-222-4148). Lara Belice is head teacher (2022 National Signature Project Award; Rural Teacher recognition). District uses Gmail addresses per Park County 2024-25 directory (cookecityclerk@gmail.com, cookecityteacher@gmail.com). Cell: (406) 946-2929. Cooke City School District #9.

title: Superintendent, Glendive Public Schools
notes: Dawson County HS in Glendive (paired with Glendive Elem; Schreibeis is district Supt). District uses glendiveschools.org/dchsglendive.com domains. Address: 900 N. Merrill Ave, Glendive, MT 59330.

title: Superintendent/Principal
notes: DeSmet School District (PK-8, Missoula County). Combined Supt/Principal role. Email verified via district staff page. Address: 6355 Padre Lane, Missoula, MT 59808. School Clerk: Katie Brubaker kbrubaker@desmetschool.org.

title: Pondera County Contracted Superintendent of Schools (oversees Dupuyer)
notes: Small Class III rural district (~46 students PK-8) in Dupuyer, Pondera County. 0 district administrators per NCES. Oversight from Pondera County Contracted Supt Jacqueline Christiaens. School address: 319 Columbus Loop, Dupuyer, MT 59432; school phone (406) 472-3297. Alt county contact: Shaunna Graham (Treasurer/Assessor/Supt), shaunna.graham@ponderacountymt.gov.

title: Superintendent
notes: Elysian School District #23 (K-8 ~400 students), Yellowstone County (Billings, MT, NOT Missoula). Address: 6416 Elysian Rd, Billings, MT 59101. Principal: Ryan Truscott. Email format inferred from elysianschool.org domain pattern.

Fortine School District (Class III, Lincoln County). Small K-8 rural school. Public sources did not surface a current named Superintendent/Principal. District is one of the Lincoln County Class III schools that may be overseen by Lincoln County Supt Suzy Rios (srios@libby.org). Address: 358 Meadow Creek Rd, PO Box 96, Fortine, MT 59918. Recommend Apollo lookup or direct call to (406) 882-4531.

title: Superintendent/Principal
notes: Gardiner Public Schools (Park County, MT). Combined Supt/Principal. Email format inferred from gardiner.org domain pattern. Address: 510 Stone Street, Gardiner, MT 59030. Bray was hired summer 2024.

title: County Superintendent (oversees Grant)
notes: Tiny one-teacher rural district (~9 students PK-8) at 11700 Hwy 324, Dillon, MT 59725. NOT to be confused with in-town Grant Elementary in Dillon. No district superintendent; overseen by Beaverhead County Supt at 2 S. Pacific St., Suite #7, Dillon, MT 59725. Email mmiller@beaverheadcounty.org from county website. School phone: (406) 681-3143.

title: Superintendent
notes: Hays-Lodge Pole K-12 Schools, Blaine County, on the Fort Belknap Indian Reservation (tribal/Head Start partnership context). Email confirmed via ZoomInfo as c***@hlpschools.k12.mt.us; cbarron format is the standard district pattern. PO Box 110, Hays, MT 59527.

title: Superintendent/Principal
notes: Independent Public School (~299 students PK-6), Yellowstone County (Billings). Chouinard serves as combined Supt/Principal per multiple sources. Email format inferred from independent.k12.mt.us domain pattern. Address: 2907 Roundup Road, Billings, MT 59105.

title: Custer County Superintendent of Schools (oversees Kircher)
notes: Kircher School District (Custer County, ~65 students PK-8). No district superintendent (0.5 district admins per NCES); overseen by Custer County Supt Carla Begger. Teachers: Shauna Livesay (K-2), Kylie Hill (3-4), Ben Hill (5-6), Tatum Lauing (7-8). Board: Houston Finn, Pat Linger, Correy Wilk; clerk Stacy Montgomery. School address: 331 Kircher Creek Rd, Miles City, MT 59301. School phone: (406) 234-2761.

title: Superintendent, Eureka Public Schools
notes: Lincoln County HS is part of Eureka Public Schools District #13. Joel Graves serves as Supt. District uses teameureka.net and lchigh.net domains (note: NOT the .k12.mt.us pattern). Central Office: 340 9th St E, PO Box 2000, Eureka, MT 59917.

title: Superintendent
notes: Lone Rock School District (~250 students K-8), Stevensville, Ravalli County. Prior Supt Robert DoBell passed May 2025; Kathy Schneider was interim. Teresa Weems was hired/introduced as new Supt for 2025-26. Email format inferred from lonerockschool.org domain pattern. Recommend verification.

McCormick School District (K-8, Lincoln County, Troy, MT). Small Class III with 2 teachers and ~20 students. No dedicated district administrator surfaced in public sources; likely overseen by Lincoln County Supt Suzy Rios (srios@libby.org). School address: 1564 Old Hwy 2 N, Troy, MT 59935. Recommend Apollo enrichment.

title: Yellowstone County Superintendent of Schools (oversees Morin)
notes: Morin School District #17 (~25-40 students K-6) in Yellowstone County south of Billings on Crow Indian Reservation (tribal context). 0 school admins per NCES; overseen by Yellowstone County Supt Hank Peters. Teachers: Kimberly Neujahr, Jennifer Smith, Susan Seastrand, Heather Mayo, Tia Schacht. Address: 8824 Pryor Road, Billings, MT 59101.

title: Superintendent
notes: Noxon Public Schools (K-12 ~200 students), Sanders County (serves Heron, Noxon, Trout Creek). Mike Ehinger is current Supt per Parents Right-To-Know page (previously at Reed Point). One source mentioned Dave Whitesell but appears outdated. Email format inferred from noxonschools.com domain. Address: 300 Noxon Ave, Noxon, MT 59853.

title: Superintendent, Livingston Public Schools
notes: Park HS is the HS in Livingston Public Schools (paired w/ Livingston Elem). Chad Johnson is district Supt. Email confirmed via ZoomInfo as c***@livingston.k12.mt.us; cjohnson format standard. District Office: 510 Stone Street, Livingston, MT 59047. Park HS: 102 View Vista Dr.

title: Superintendent/Principal
notes: Pioneer School District 41 (PK-8 ~84 students), Yellowstone County (Billings). Combined Supt/Principal. Email confirmed via ZoomInfo s***@pioneerschool.us with format schnitzmeier@pioneerschool.us. Address: 1937 Dover Rd, Billings, MT 59105.

district: Powder River Co Dist H S
title: Superintendent/Principal, Broadus Public Schools
notes: Powder River County District HS in Broadus (paired w/ Broadus Elem). Sarah Shannon serves as Supt/Principal. District was actively seeking dedicated Supt to begin July 1, 2025 - status may have changed. Email format inferred from broadus.net domain pattern. District clerk: Kim Blain.

title: Administrator
notes: Ramsay School District #3 (small elementary/middle in Butte-Silver Bow County). Administrator contact kelloggc@ramsay.k12.mt.us per district sources. Last name Kellogg; first name not fully surfaced - recommend verification. Address: PO Box 115 - 3 Russell St., Ramsay, MT 59748. Main school: (406) 723-3656.

title: Superintendent
notes: Rocky Boy Public Schools (District 87 J&L), Box Elder, MT - on Rocky Boy's Indian Reservation (tribal/Head Start partnership context). Voyd St. Pierre is long-serving Supt and elected NAFIS Board director. Email confirmed via ZoomInfo as v***@rockyboy.k12.mt.us; vstpierre format inferred. Address: 81 Mission Taylor Rd, Box Elder, MT 59521. Main district phone: (406) 395-4474.

title: Superintendent
notes: Shepherd Public Schools (K-12, Yellowstone County). O'Donnell previously served as Curriculum Director for Shepherd Public Schools (relevant for ECE outreach). Email format inferred from district pattern lastname+firstinitial@shepherd.k12.mt.us. Fax: 373-5284.

title: Lead Teacher
notes: Tiny Class III rural district (~16 students PK-8, 2 teachers) in Gallatin County. No superintendent or principal - Taylor Woller is Lead Teacher. Overseen by Gallatin County Supt. Address: 6020 Springhill Community Rd, Belgrade, MT 59714. Recommend Apollo enrichment for email.

title: Lead Teacher (no Supt; oversight by Missoula County Supt)
notes: Tiny Class III one-room school (~11 students PK-8) at 5024 Sunset Hill Rd, Greenough, MT 59823, Missoula County. Toni Hatten was 2023 Montana Rural Teacher of the Year - lead teacher there. No district Supt; oversight by Missoula County Supt (406-258-4860). Recommend Apollo enrichment or call to Missoula County Supt office.

title: Superintendent
notes: Target Range School District #23 (K-8), Missoula County. Corey Austin was 2015 MT Superintendent of the Year (PhD Indiana State). Email confirmed via ZoomInfo as ****@target.k12.mt.us; caustin format standard. Address: 4095 South Ave W, Missoula, MT 59804.

title: Superintendent

title: Superintendent
notes: West Yellowstone K-12 (Gallatin County). Email confirmed via district staff page. Alt email: kflanagan@wolverine.life. Address: 411 N. Geyser, PO Box 460, West Yellowstone, MT 59758. Main phone: (406) 646-7617.

title: Superintendent, Lolo School District (associated; Woodman is Class III rural)
notes: Woodman School District (Class III rural K-8 in Lolo, Missoula County). Public sources list Dale Olinger as Lolo School Supt (referenced for the area) and 'Mr. Martin' as principal at Woodman, but Woodman is a separate district overseen by Missoula County Supt office (406-258-4860). Mountain rural school, ~30 students. Recommend Apollo enrichment for direct staff contact and verification of current principal/teacher.
